author: Emberleif
description: A Gantt-style timeboard for project-based time tracking across notes. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Emberleif
manifest.description: A Gantt-style timeboard for project-based time tracking across tasks.
manifest.name: Chronoboard

name: Chronoboard

releases.author: Emberleif
releases.description:
Chronoboard 0.7.0 focuses on onboarding, documentation, and workflow clarity.

### Highlights
- Added a managed getting started guide inside the vault:
  - `Getting Started With Chronoboard`
- Added a managed changelog note inside the vault:
  - `Chronoboard - Changelog`
- New users now get the guide opened automatically on first install
- Updating users now get the changelog opened automatically once per version
- Added command palette actions for opening the guide and changelog
- Added a compact `?` help icon in the task panel
- Added clearer setup guidance around required status frontmatter
- Improved totals header presentation and sort control behavior
- Improved time entry note handling so renamed notes still reopen correctly through aliases and entry IDs
- Added template support for time entry notes with Chronoboard fields appended after template frontmatter

### Clarified interactions
- Double click a time block to remove it
- Hold and drag a time block to move it
- Use `Ctrl+Z` or `Cmd+Z` to undo adding or removing a time block
- Removing a task from the left rail does not remove existing tracked time from the board

### Setup note
Chronoboard reads tasks from a configured folder and expects them to use your configured frontmatter property, `Status` by default, unless they are explicitly included through `Always include tasks`.
